claims.statement: SelfConnect can inject visible terminal input through the Win32 WM_CHAR path without requiring MCP, HTTP, or a cloud API for that actuation step.
claims.scope: Windows terminal surfaces accepted by the guarded package path.
claims.boundary: This does not claim universal application input or that the wider ecosystem never uses APIs, MCP, brokers, or networks.

claims.statement: A UIA TextChanged event was observed on the tested Windows Terminal ConPTY surface, and echo filtering separated injected echo from external output.
claims.scope: The recorded throwaway Windows Terminal surface and reusable echo-filter helper.
claims.boundary: This is not a claim of UIA event parity across every terminal, application, Windows build, or accessibility implementation.

claims.statement: SelfConnect read and controlled an owned local page in an isolated Microsoft Edge profile through UIA and stopped at a mock protected checkpoint.
claims.scope: Microsoft Edge against the recorded local owned fixture.
claims.boundary: This does not claim public-site, multi-tab, Chrome, Firefox, CAPTCHA bypass, anti-bot evasion, download, upload, passkey, or cross-browser parity.

claims.statement: The recorded five-logical-agent Fabric V2 service-transport run measured transport/governance p99 of 1.049 ms, end-to-end p99 of 1.840 ms, audit p99 of 0.464 ms, and zero model calls for known tasks.
claims.scope: The exact recorded logical workload, machine, profiles, transport, and artifact.
claims.boundary: This is not real-model latency, a universal hardware result, an installed-service claim, or proof that Fabric V2 is the current default runtime.

claims.statement: The recorded Fabric V2 user-mode service-wrapper selftest passed roundtrip, restart, state-file, and watchdog checks.
claims.scope: The Python user-mode service-wrapper selftest using the Windows AF_PIPE path.
claims.boundary: This does not prove an installed, always-running Windows SCM service on every target machine or full governed daemon coverage.

claims.statement: A portable release claim for the twenty-real-agent ladder is not yet approved because the raw local evidence is intentionally ignored and no tracked redacted run artifact is attached to this ledger.
claims.boundary: Do not promote the twenty-real-agent result into portable release positioning until a reviewed redacted artifact is tracked and hashed.
A TPM capability probe or TPM-backed signing must not be described as remote-verifiable platform attestation.

claims.statement: The local selfconnect-enterprise checkout did not have a zero-failure test result on 2026-07-09: 1,305 tests passed, 2 security gates failed, and 21 were skipped at commit 9d3f98d.
claims.scope: Local selfconnect-enterprise commit 9d3f98d and the installed dependency environment.
claims.boundary: Do not state 716 tests with zero failures, or any other zero-failure count, until the exact release commit and environment are rerun and their artifact is recorded. The local checkout was four commits behind origin/master.

claims.statement: A governed end-to-end ApprovalRelay-to-Telegram-to-target-guard roundtrip is not yet proven in the release evidence set.
claims.scope: Existing local approval and Telegram bridge prototypes.
claims.boundary: The current scripts must not be described as one integrated governed primitive: the escalation file is not consumed by approval_telegram.py, the bridge selects a broadly matching terminal, and reply injection does not use the full package target guard.

claims.statement: SelfConnect does not make a release claim that no prior art replicates its architecture or that a specific method is patentable.
claims.boundary: Novelty, non-obviousness, claim scope, and patentability require a documented prior-art search and patent-counsel review. Technical differentiation may be described only as a scoped engineering comparison.

claims.statement: Fabric V2 is not yet the default SelfConnect runtime data plane.
claims.boundary: Do not claim default deployment or always-on operation until installation, startup, watchdog, rollback, and live workload gates pass on the release candidate.
